serving.natural_language_portion: One standard 330 ml can of 7UP, as typically served in Singapore and food delivery platforms.

nutrient_source.title: 7UP Lemon Lime Soda, USDA FoodData Central, FDC ID 1104261
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA FoodData Central Branded Food entry for 7UP lemon-lime carbonated soft drink used as primary nutrient source. Values are per 100 g (approximately 100 ml for this beverage). Singapore canned formulation may differ marginally in sweetener type but macronutrient profile is consistent with the USDA branded entry.

gi_source.title: Soft drink, regular, carbonated (lemon-lime type) — Glycemic Index entry
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for 7UP brand specifically. GI of 51 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for regular lemon-lime carbonated soft drink (glucose reference = 100). Glycemic load per 330 ml serving calculated as: (51 × 35.0 g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 17.85, rounded to 18.5. Carbohydrate per serving = 10.6 g per 100 g × 3.3 = 34.98 g ≈ 35.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (11 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: 7UP contains only 11 mg sodium per 100 g, making it a low-sodium beverage; however, its high sugar content may contribute to weight gain and secondary blood pressure elevation, so individuals with hypertension should limit consumption.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High free sugar content (10.6 g per 100 g; approximately 35 g per 330 ml can)
type2_diabetes.guidance: 7UP delivers approximately 35 g of rapidly absorbed free sugars per standard 330 ml can, causing a significant postprandial glucose spike; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption and consider sugar-free alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake linked to elevated triglycerides and cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages such as 7UP is associated with elevated serum triglycerides and increased cardiovascular disease risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should restrict intake in line with WHO guidance on free sugar consumption (less than 10% of total energy intake).

llm_training.natural_language_description: 7UP is a globally recognised lemon-lime flavoured carbonated soft drink manufactured by Keurig Dr Pepper (formerly PepsiCo in some markets). It is sold in cans, bottles, and fountain format worldwide, including Singapore, where it is commonly available at hawker centres, restaurants, and via food delivery platforms. The drink is clear, effervescent, and characterised by a crisp, sweet citrus flavour derived from natural lemon and lime flavourings, citric acid, and potassium citrate. It contains no caffeine. Per 100 g, 7UP provides approximately 40 kcal, 10.6 g of carbohydrate (entirely as free sugars), 0 g protein, and 0 g fat. A standard 330 ml can delivers approximately 132 kcal and 35 g of free sugars. Protein and fat are absent by product formulation (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central). The beverage is ultra-processed and classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB). From a clinical nutrition perspective, 7UP is of high concern for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its rapid glycaemic impact from free sugars, and of moderate concern for cardiovascular disease due to the association between SSB consumption and elevated triglycerides. Its sodium content is low (11 mg per 100 g), posing minimal direct hypertension risk, though habitual SSB consumption may contribute to obesity-related hypertension. The glycaemic index is estimated at 51 (low GI by threshold), but the glycaemic load per 330 ml serving is approximately 18.5, which is clinically significant. 7UP is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal or gluten-free diets. It contains no known allergens.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a can of 7UP?
What is the sugar content of 7UP?
Can I drink 7UP if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central branded food entry for 7UP lemon-lime carbonated soft drink (FDC ID 1104261), which is an approved primary source for packaged commercial products. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for regular lemon-lime carbonated soft drink. All mandatory multilingual names and aliases are present. Ontology codes are defensible closest-parent matches with documented gap notes.
notes: Protein_g is 0.0: contains no protein — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; 7UP is a sugar-water carbonated beverage with no protein by formulation. Fat_g is 0.0: contains no fat — confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central; 7UP contains no fat by formulation. Saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and dietary_fibre_g are all 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central for this product category. Calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central source entry for this product. Iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. Glycemic_index set to 51 (low GI, ≤55 threshold) based on University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for regular lemon-lime carbonated soft drink; gi_category is low per the ≤55 threshold rule. Glycemic_load_per_serving of 18.5 calculated for a 330 ml standard can serving (35.0 g carbohydrate × GI 51 / 100 = 17.85, rounded to 18.5). The Singapore retail formulation of 7UP may use cane sugar rather than HFCS; macronutrient profile remains consistent. Input food_name '7Up Canned Drink' normalised to canonical product name '7UP Canned Carbonated Soft Drink'. Input cuisine tag 'The Noodle Place - Circular Road' reflects the field survey restaurant context and is not a cuisine category; cuisine set to 'Global / International'. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a globally distributed product sold in Singapore.
